summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the ExecuteCommand workflow node executes arbitrary shell commands without validation. The PoC creates a workflow with an ExecuteCommand node containing an arbitrary command, activates the workflow, and executes it to demonstrate successful command injection.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause n8n to execute the command 'echo EVOHUNT_POC_COMMAND_INJECTION_SUCCESSFUL > /tmp/poc_output.txt' via the ExecuteCommand node, creating a proof file demonstrating arbitrary command execution.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully built n8n from source, created a workflow with ExecuteCommand node, activated it, and executed the workflow. The command was executed and created /tmp/poc_output.txt with the expected proof string, confirming arbitrary command execution without validation.
verification_method: The reproduction script checks for the existence of /tmp/poc_output.txt and verifies it contains the expected proof string 'EVOHUNT_POC_COMMAND_INJECTION_SUCCESSFUL'. This distinguishes successful command execution from workflow execution failures or startup errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: Workflow with ExecuteCommand node successfully created via REST API
Workflow activation succeeded without validation errors
Manual workflow execution triggered the command
Proof file /tmp/poc_output.txt created with expected content
Command executed with full shell access on the host system

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the master branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
